name: Wireframe Mode
description: CSS theme that transforms GoA Design System applications into grayscale wireframe appearance for early-stage validation and stakeholder feedback. Visual fidelity signals commitment - wireframe mode says 'let's shape this together' instead of 'this is decided.'
summary: A standalone CSS file that overrides GoA component styling to create a sketch-like appearance. Use it to get structural feedback instead of detail-focused feedback during early validation.

coreInsight.problem: Production components look 'done', which signals commitment and invites nitpicky feedback on colors and details instead of structural feedback on flow and content
coreInsight.solution: CSS theme switching - same functional prototype renders at different visual fidelity levels
coreInsight.benefit: Match visual fidelity to conversation stage. Early exploration in wireframe, final validation in production.

whenToUse: Getting feedback on flow and structure (not visual details)
Running usability tests focused on navigation and layout
Presenting to people who might think a prototype is 'done'
Designer exploration before committing to final styling
Product owner communicating ideas to team
whenNotToUse: Accessibility testing that requires color contrast validation

visualChanges.typography: System fonts replace brand fonts (system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif)
visualChanges.borderRadius: Minimal corners (2px) for boxy, structural appearance
visualChanges.borders: Dashed borders on form inputs and containers
visualChanges.images: Grayscale filter with reduced opacity on all images, logos, SVGs
visualChanges.banner: Fixed 'Wireframe - Not final design' banner at top of page

whatStaysSame: All spacing and layout (structure must remain accurate for testing)

setup.description: Add wireframe mode to any GoA Design System application in 2 minutes
steps: 1. Download wireframe-theme.css and add to your project
2. Link the CSS file in your HTML head
3. Add the keyboard shortcut toggle (Cmd+. or Ctrl+.)

fileLocations.plain-html: Same directory as HTML or assets/ folder

keyboardToggle.description: Recommended: Cmd+. (Mac) or Ctrl+. (Windows) to toggle

checkboxToggle.code:
<label style="position: fixed; bottom: 16px; right: 16px; z-index: 9999; background: white; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 4px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); cursor: pointer;">
  <input type="checkbox" onchange="document.documentElement.dataset.fidelity = this.checked ? 'wireframe' : ''" />
  Wireframe mode
</label>

startInWireframe.description: Start app in wireframe mode by default

urlParameter.description: Toggle via URL for sharing wireframe views

wireframeModeDoesNotApply: Check CSS is loaded: Open browser dev tools > Network tab > verify wireframe-theme.css loaded
Check data attribute: Inspect <html> element - should have data-fidelity="wireframe"
Check CSS order: wireframe-theme.css should load AFTER @abgov/web-components/index.css
troubleshooting.someComponentsNotFullyStyled: Components using Shadow DOM may not fully transform. The CSS applies grayscale filters to entire Shadow DOM components (like goa-app-header) as a workaround.
keyboardShortcutDoesNotWork: Make sure no browser extension or app is capturing Cmd+. / Ctrl+.
Add console.log to verify event listener was added
Try the checkbox toggle as an alternative
troubleshooting.logoStaysColored: Ensure wireframe-theme.css includes the Shadow DOM component filter rules for goa-app-header and goa-work-side-menu

feedbackSignaling.concept: Visual fidelity signals commitment level to stakeholders
feedbackSignaling.keyInsight: Showing polished UI too early pulls feedback toward details when you need feedback on fundamentals

steps: 1. Describe concept and generate prototype with MCP
2. Enable wireframe mode (Cmd+.)
3. Walk through with stakeholders
4. Get structural feedback ('should payment come before or after review?')
5. Iterate on wireframe
6. When structure is validated, disable wireframe mode
7. Get final sign-off on polished version
1. Get design concept from team
2. Generate prototype
3. Enable wireframe mode
4. Tell participants: 'This is an early concept, we're deciding how it should work'
5. Get genuine structural feedback (not 'I don't like that shade of blue')
6. Report findings to team
1. Generate form wizard in wireframe mode
2. See structural flow without GoA styling distractions
3. Compare with alternative approach
4. Pick preferred structure
5. Disable wireframe mode to see with real components
6. Finalize design decisions

productOwners: Get structural feedback, not detail bikeshedding
Stakeholders more willing to suggest changes
researchers: Participants give honest feedback on flows
Can test rough concepts, not just finished designs
developers: Understand intent before polish is applied
Can see what's structural vs. decorative

technicalDetails.mechanism: CSS custom property overrides scoped to data attribute
technicalDetails.performance: No JavaScript required for styling - pure CSS
